metadata.description: Required. Display name for the workbook.
Required. Azure region for the workbook resource.
Required. Source resource ID for workbook queries (for example, an Application Insights component ID).
Optional. Description shown in the workbook metadata.
Optional. Tags to apply to the workbook resource.

content.json:
# Search Index Worker Monitoring
Monitors throughput, failures, latency, sync lag, and worker liveness telemetry from SearchIndexWorker.

content.query:
customMetrics
| where name in ('search.batch.documents.indexed', 'search.batch.documents.removed', 'search.batch.documents.skipped')
| extend metricValue = todouble(coalesce(valueSum, value))
| summarize total = sum(metricValue) by name, bin(timestamp, 5m)
| render timechart
content.title: Batch throughput (indexed / removed / skipped)

content.query:
customMetrics
| where name in ('search.batch.failures', 'search.indexing.failures')
| extend metricValue = todouble(coalesce(valueSum, value))
| summarize total = sum(metricValue) by name, bin(timestamp, 5m)
| render timechart
content.title: Failure metrics

content.query:
customMetrics
| where name == 'search.embedding.latency.ms'
| extend metricValue = todouble(coalesce(valueSum, value))
| summarize p50 = percentile(metricValue, 50), p95 = percentile(metricValue, 95), p99 = percentile(metricValue, 99) by bin(timestamp, 5m)
| render timechart
content.title: Embedding latency (p50/p95/p99)

content.query:
customMetrics
| where name == 'search.sync.lag.seconds'
| extend metricValue = todouble(coalesce(valueSum, value))
| summarize p50 = percentile(metricValue, 50), p95 = percentile(metricValue, 95) by bin(timestamp, 5m)
| render timechart
content.title: Sync lag (seconds, p50/p95)

content.query:
customMetrics
| where name == 'search.worker.alive'
| extend metricValue = todouble(coalesce(valueSum, value))
| summarize minAlive = min(metricValue), maxAlive = max(metricValue) by bin(timestamp, 5m)
| render timechart
content.title: Worker liveness (0 = stopped, 1 = running)

metadata.description: The resource ID of the workbook.
